rounds_summary.attempted_route: biopsykit.sleep.sleep_processing_pipeline.predict_pipeline_acceleration(acc_data) — sampling_rate arg omitted; acc columns stripped of 'acc_' prefix
rounds_summary.observation: TypeError: predict_pipeline_acceleration() missing 1 required positional argument: 'sampling_rate'. Script crashed at line 34 in 1.8 s; no output produced.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: predict_pipeline_acceleration(acc_data, sampling_rate=sampling_rate) with sampling_rate inferred from median inter-sample interval; tz-aware DatetimeIndex kept as-is
rounds_summary.observation: OutOfBoundsDatetime inside biopsykit.signals.imu.rest_periods._resample_index: pd.to_datetime(values, unit='us') overflows on nanosecond .asi8 values (~1.57e18).

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Same as round 2 but strips timezone via tz_convert('UTC').tz_localize(None) before calling predict_pipeline_acceleration, hoping to lower .asi8 magnitudes
rounds_summary.observation: Identical OutOfBoundsDatetime overflow (same value 1.567469178251953e+18). tz_localize(None) does not change nanosecond .asi8 integers; crash recurs unchanged.

ruled_out_routes: predict_pipeline_acceleration(acc_data) without sampling_rate — mandatory positional arg, always raises TypeError
predict_pipeline_acceleration with tz-aware DatetimeIndex — BioPsyKit _resample_index reinterprets nanosecond .asi8 as microseconds, guaranteed OutOfBoundsDatetime overflow
tz_convert('UTC').tz_localize(None) as overflow fix — produces naive UTC DatetimeIndex but .asi8 values are unchanged nanosecond integers; overflow persists identically
synthesised_diagnosis: All three failures stem from a single incompatibility: the dataset's DatetimeIndex stores epoch values as nanoseconds (~1.57e18), but BioPsyKit's _resample_index unconditionally calls pd.to_datetime(raw_integers, unit='us'), treating them as microseconds. This overflows pandas' datetime range. Round 1 never reached this code; rounds 2 and 3 both hit it. Round 3's timezone-stripping did not help because tz status does not affect .asi8 magnitude — the fix must rescale the integer values themselves.
rationale: BioPsyKit's _resample_index hard-codes unit='us' when converting index integers; nanosecond-resolution pandas DatetimeIndex always overflows this conversion regardless of timezone. No BioPsyKit API parameter controls this behaviour, so the index must be downsampled to microsecond resolution before the call.
recommended_alternative: Reconstruct the DatetimeIndex at microsecond resolution before calling predict_pipeline_acceleration: compute epoch_us = (data.index.asi8 // 1000), then data.index = pd.to_datetime(epoch_us, unit='us', utc=True).tz_localize(None). This rescales integers from nanoseconds to microseconds so BioPsyKit's pd.to_datetime(..., unit='us') call stays within bounds. Then infer sampling_rate and call predict_pipeline_acceleration(acc_data, sampling_rate=sampling_rate) as before.
